grounding_note: Expansion batch: more marital-status and arrangement-structure disclosures.

scenarios.opening_query: My partner of nine years just died and his family is taking everything, including things we bought together.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Probate || Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements
scenarios.hidden_fact: We never married or registered anything — no will either — so I'm told I have no automatic inheritance rights and my claims rest on proving what I paid for and contributed.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We never married or registered, and there's no will — so my claims rest on proving what I paid for and contributed.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether any legal relationship or will gives the survivor standing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We registered as domestic partners years ago — that gives me spousal-equivalent inheritance rights in his estate case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We're registered domestic partners — that gives me spousal-equivalent rights in the estate.
scenarios.vetting_note: Survivor-standing fact; the hardest real consequence of the unmarried gap.

scenarios.opening_query: My girlfriend and I are buying a house together and her mother keeps warning us we're being naive.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > General (Residential) || Family Law > Prenuptial Agreements || Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements
scenarios.hidden_fact: We're engaged, wedding next summer — the smart move we're told is a prenup covering the house and our uneven down payments, alongside the purchase.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We're engaged — wedding next summer — so it's really a prenup covering the house and our uneven down payments.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an upcoming marriage makes premarital agreement work the need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No marriage plans at all — we need a cohabitation and co-ownership agreement that protects us both if we split.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No marriage plans — we need a cohabitation and co-ownership agreement in case we split.
scenarios.vetting_note: Engagement status selects prenup vs cohabitation-agreement instruments.

scenarios.opening_query: I've supported my partner's kids like my own for six years and now that we're separating she says I have no rights to them.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements || Family Law > Grandparent/Third Party Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: The kids question is what matters to me — I'm told our state lets a third party with a parent-child relationship petition for visitation, and that's what I want to pursue.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the kids that matter — our state lets a third party with a real parent-child relationship petition for visitation, and I want to pursue that.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether third-party visitation rights are the goal
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Honestly the fight is the house and accounts we mixed for six years — the kids' mom and I have worked out contact informally.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The real fight is the house and mixed accounts — contact with the kids we've worked out ourselves.
scenarios.vetting_note: Relief-sought fact: psychological-parent visitation vs property untangling.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex moved out three years ago and I just found out she's been running up debt in both our names.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements || Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Consumer Law > Identity Theft
scenarios.hidden_fact: We're still legally married — never filed anything — so every 'both our names' account ties back to the marriage, and the divorce case is where it all gets divided.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We're still legally married — never filed — so those joint accounts tie back to the marriage, and the divorce is where it gets divided.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an undissolved marriage frames the debt problem
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We were never married — and the 'joint' accounts are ones she opened by forging my signature after she left. That's identity theft.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We were never married — she opened those accounts forging my signature after leaving. That's identity theft.
scenarios.vetting_note: Marriage status turns the same debt discovery into property division or fraud.

scenarios.opening_query: My late brother's girlfriend refuses to leave his house and we're supposed to be settling his affairs.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Probate || Real Property > Landlord Residential || Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements
scenarios.hidden_fact: It turns out she's a bona fide tenant — she's paid him documented monthly rent for four years, so the estate has to treat her as a tenant with eviction protections, not a guest.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She's actually a documented tenant — four years of monthly rent payments — so the estate has to follow eviction law, not just ask her to go.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the occupant has tenant status against the estate
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She never paid anything — she moved in months before he died, and the estate's administration is where her claims get sorted.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She never paid anything and moved in just months before he died — it's for the estate process to sort.
scenarios.vetting_note: Rent-history fact gives the occupant tenancy rights the estate must respect.

scenarios.opening_query: We did one of those commitment ceremonies years ago and now that we're splitting nobody can tell us what we actually are.
scenarios.final: Family Law > Same Sex Couples and Parents
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Family Law > Same Sex Couples and Parents || Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements
scenarios.hidden_fact: We're a same-sex couple whose ceremony predates marriage recognition — whether the state retroactively treats us as married, plus our son born in between, is exactly the specialty question.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We're a same-sex couple — the ceremony predates recognition, and with our son born in between, whether we're retroactively married is the whole question.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether pre-recognition relationship history creates specialized status questions
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was symbolic only and we knew it — never licensed, never registered; it's a straightforward unmarried split.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was symbolic and we knew it — never licensed or registered. Just an unmarried split.
scenarios.vetting_note: Pre-Obergefell status questions are the taxonomy label's core use.
